nodes.description: Nonspecific brain abnormalities on head imaging are reported in Ayme-Gripp syndrome and can accompany developmental delay and seizures.

evidence.snippet: Other features may include postnatal short stature, seizure disorder, nonspecific brain abnormalities on head imaging, skeletal abnormalities, and joint limitations.

nodes.description: Brachycephaly is one of the characteristic craniofacial findings in Ayme-Gripp syndrome.

evidence.snippet: The facial features are often described as "Down syndrome-like" and include brachycephaly, flat facial appearance, short nose, long philtrum, narrow mouth, and low-set and posteriorly rotated ears.

nodes.description: Congenital or early-onset bilateral cataracts are a cardinal feature of Ayme-Gripp syndrome.

evidence.snippet: Aymé-Gripp syndrome is classically defined as the triad of bilateral early cataracts, sensorineural hearing loss, and characteristic facial features in combination with neurodevelopmental abnormalities.

nodes.description: Cataracts are treated with ophthalmologic surgery when indicated, with ongoing refraction and ophthalmology follow-up for refractive error and other visual complications.

evidence.snippet: Hearing aids or cochlear implant for sensorineural hearing loss; surgical intervention and eye glasses for cataracts and refractive errors, respectively

nodes.description: Dental anomalies and malocclusion have been reported in rare affected individuals and are supported by model-organism data.

evidence.snippet: Other features including gastrointestinal and endocrine abnormalities, ectodermal dysplasia (i.e., nail dystrophy and mammary gland hypoplasia), dental anomalies, and chronic glomerulopathy with proteinuria have been reported in rare affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Generation and mutational analysis of a transgenic murine model of the human MAF mutation.
evidence.snippet: The murine model showed decreased brain volume and malocclusion.

nodes.description: Developmental delay and variable cognitive impairment are managed with early intervention, physical therapy, speech-language therapy, occupational therapy, and individualized educational supports.

evidence.snippet: standard therapy for developmental delay / cognitive impairment, seizure disorder, scoliosis, congenital heart defects / pericardial issues, oligodontia, and hypothyroidism.

nodes.description: A flat facial profile is part of the recognizable facial gestalt of Ayme-Gripp syndrome.

evidence.snippet: The facial features are often described as "Down syndrome-like" and include brachycephaly, flat facial appearance, short nose, long philtrum, narrow mouth, and low-set and posteriorly rotated ears.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is universal or near-universal across reported individuals, although severity varies.

evidence.snippet: All affected individuals have had developmental delay, but the degree of cognitive impairment is extremely variable.

nodes.description: Postnatal short stature and poor somatic growth are part of the syndrome's multisystem phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Other features may include postnatal short stature, seizure disorder, nonspecific brain abnormalities on head imaging, skeletal abnormalities, and joint limitations.

nodes.description: Hearing aids are used for sensorineural hearing loss, with cochlear implant evaluation when hearing loss is severe or inadequately managed by hearing aids.

evidence.snippet: Hearing aids or cochlear implant for sensorineural hearing loss; surgical intervention and eye glasses for cataracts and refractive errors, respectively

nodes.description: Pathogenic missense variants in the MAF transactivation domain disrupt the conserved GSK3 phosphorylation motifs that normally regulate MAF turnover.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations Impairing GSK3-Mediated MAF Phosphorylation Cause Cataract, Deafness, Intellectual Disability, Seizures, and a Down Syndrome-like Facies.
evidence.snippet: Seven different de novo missense mutations involving conserved residues of the four GSK3 phosphorylation motifs were identified in eight unrelated individuals.

meta.association: Pathogenic missense variants impairing phosphorylation-dependent turnover

evidence.reference_title: Mutations Impairing GSK3-Mediated MAF Phosphorylation Cause Cataract, Deafness, Intellectual Disability, Seizures, and a Down Syndrome-like Facies.
evidence.snippet: Seven different de novo missense mutations involving conserved residues of the four GSK3 phosphorylation motifs were identified in eight unrelated individuals.

nodes.description: Dysregulated MAF activity affects multiple organ systems, producing cataract, hearing loss, abnormal skull growth, developmental impairment, and other ectodermal and skeletal features.
nodes.id: Multisystem developmental defects in lens, ear, brain, and growth

evidence.reference_title: Generation and mutational analysis of a transgenic murine model of the human MAF mutation.
evidence.snippet: Our murine model exhibited similar phenotypes to those in humans, such as lens abnormalities, short stature, growth retardation, and abnormal skull morphology.

nodes.description: Pericarditis or pericardial effusion can occur in the neonatal or infantile period in a subset of individuals with Ayme-Gripp syndrome.

evidence.snippet: A subset of individuals have been found to have pericarditis or pericardial effusion during the neonatal or infantile period.

nodes.description: Seizures are a recurrent neurologic feature in Ayme-Gripp syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations Impairing GSK3-Mediated MAF Phosphorylation Cause Cataract, Deafness, Intellectual Disability, Seizures, and a Down Syndrome-like Facies.
evidence.snippet: Seizures and abnormal EEG findings consistent with focal and diffuse abnormal activity were present in all individuals, most often diagnosed in early childhood.

nodes.description: Seizures are managed with standard antiseizure therapy and neurologic follow-up, including EEG or imaging when clinically indicated.

evidence.snippet: standard therapy for developmental delay / cognitive impairment, seizure disorder, scoliosis, congenital heart defects / pericardial issues, oligodontia, and hypothyroidism.

nodes.description: Hearing loss is usually congenital or recognized early in life and forms part of the classic diagnostic triad.

evidence.snippet: Hearing loss is often congenital.

nodes.description: Skeletal abnormalities and limitation of joint mobility are recurring musculoskeletal manifestations.

evidence.snippet: Other features may include postnatal short stature, seizure disorder, nonspecific brain abnormalities on head imaging, skeletal abnormalities, and joint limitations.

nodes.description: Failure of MAF phosphorylation impairs ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation, producing an abnormally persistent transcription factor that perturbs developmental gene-expression programs.
nodes.id: Stabilized MAF protein and dysregulated transcriptional programs

evidence.reference_title: Mutations Impairing GSK3-Mediated MAF Phosphorylation Cause Cataract, Deafness, Intellectual Disability, Seizures, and a Down Syndrome-like Facies.
evidence.snippet: Disease-causing mutations were demonstrated to impair proper MAF phosphorylation, ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation, perturbed gene expression in primary skin fibroblasts

evidence.reference_title: Mutations Impairing GSK3-Mediated MAF Phosphorylation Cause Cataract, Deafness, Intellectual Disability, Seizures, and a Down Syndrome-like Facies.
evidence.snippet: induced neurodevelopmental defects in an in vivo model.
